What is up everybody? So let's get into the Year of Change program. It's amazing. It's a sale. We are going to walk through the overview and then after this we will get into the details of exactly how you do it. But first let's talk about what this whole training lesson is going to cover. What the Year of Change program is, how the sale will be positioned, how and when to pitch the sale, fulfillment, the six-week challenge members portal, and live sale examples. So let's dive right into it with the overview of the Year of Change. It is a 12-month prepaid membership that is stacked with value and discounted. Okay, so on top of your normal membership there is going to be a few things that we're adding and then we're going to discount that entire year so they give you all of that 12 months worth of membership fees in advance. So the suggested price point is $19.99 or $14.99 depending on the front end offer that you sold. So let's dig into that. The sale is made after they've already committed to a 12-month membership if you close them at the abandoned hope already or if you sign them up directly to a 12-month membership on the Challenge Orientation Day then you can sell this at the abandoned hope. So basically the point is before we ask them for 12 months worth of membership fees we want to get them committed to a 12-month period first. Okay, once they're committed to it at a different day at a later time we'll ask them to exchange that for 12 months in advance and I'll be showing you exactly why they would take that offer. So you can then use this back end cash to finance the acquisition of more leads and more challengers essentially. So if you're able to get $1,500 in advance that should cash flow you for the next batch of challengers and you continue that process. So this works well with the Green Play or with the standard challenge deposit. Either method and we'll be going over how to deploy this for each one of those methods. So let's start off with the challenge deposit method. Here's kind of how it would work. So step one, they pay you let's just say $4.99 for the challenge. Step two, you apply that deposit over 12 months at the abandoned hope meeting. So at their halfway meeting we do the normal abandoned hope training that's in this portal and you follow that process to a T and you secure their membership over the course of the next 12 months. Now assuming that your membership is around $200 a month you have just secured $2,000 tentatively from them over the next 12 months. They owe you two grand. Step three, you are going to give them that next year for $14.99 instead and you are going to value stack on top of it. So that's how this works for the challenge deposit. For the Green Play, here's how it's going to work. Step one, they come in, they start paying you $49 a week immediately for the challenge as a new member month to month no commitment. So you're not going to need the $294 or the $296 or however you sell it upfront because you're going to get it on the backend. So this should make it much easier to sign somebody up for your challenge because they just need to give you $49 because guess what in three weeks you're going to exchange that $49 for $39 per week. Why? Because they're going to give you a 12 month agreement. So you sign them up for a 12 month agreement at $39 per week and now they're back to owing you $2,000 over the next year. Okay? But that's not it. On way out, you are going to exchange that $39 per week for $29 per week as long as it's prepaid. Now we're back to $14.99 that they're giving you in cash and you're going to value stack it. This is created an irresistible offer that they can't say no to unless they don't have the money. But you can overcome obstacles. So why are we doing this? Either way within six weeks you've gotten $2,000 from cold traffic while guaranteeing that minimum lifetime value over 12 months. So could it be more if you just collected $49 a week for 12 months? Yes. However, we all know that with normal retention rates, people don't stay for 12 months. So what we're doing here is we're guaranteeing that 12 months. We're possibly guaranteeing like a double the lifetime value. Maybe if they normally only stick for six, we're guaranteeing double the lifetime value in exchange for $2,000 roughly total. With the Green Play, it comes out to about $1,900 because you're only collecting like three-ish from the first six weeks with the deposit method. You're collecting full $2,000 when you break it down, which depending on your volume, let's just say that you have 50 challengers that you're running this month as an example and you close 10% of them. That's five challengers and they paid you $2,000 a piece. That's $10,000. That should finance your entire ad spend for the next month and you just keep doing that over and over. Meanwhile, you're building your EFT from everyone else that didn't select this offer. Pro tip, to get maximum amount of people scheduled so you can pitch this, when they sign up for your challenge, schedule their weigh-in. When they finish their weigh-in, schedule their halfway meeting. So whether you have like a digital scheduler like Acuity or Schedule 1, so whatever you're using, book the appointment. When they finish their halfway meeting, book the appointment for their weigh-out. So that way, they're always, whenever they leave a meeting, they know when they're coming back for the next meeting. Don't forget about appointment reminders. So you can send appointment reminders, you can automate it, you can call them, whatever you do, make sure they get the appointment renders just like you would with a normal lead. We still want to work those to make sure they show up and you don't get no showed for this. So that's the overview of what we're selling. Now we are going to dive into all of the details on how we're going to present it and how we're going to make it happen. See you guys there.
